mechanism_narrative: A 6-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts signals a weakening supply environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month increase in payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening labor periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the CPI signals a weakening inflation environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening inflation periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 4-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply environment that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in weakening supply periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening labor periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP and a 3-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening demand environment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
